enriched_points.expanded_content: Community resilience is measured using indicators across categories: infrastructure, social, economic, and institutional, assessing vulnerability, recovery capacity, and overall preparedness.
Tools like the ARC-D toolkit provide specific indicators to quantify resilience, aiding communities in understanding strengths and weaknesses in disaster preparedness and recovery efforts.
Implementing feedback systems ensures communities can evaluate resilience initiatives, adapt strategies, and enhance recovery processes based on real-time data and community input.
